topic: An artist discovers someone has tattooed their work
raw_output:
[story 1]

The studio air hung thick and still, smelling faintly of turpentine and old coffee. Elias stared at the canvas, the charcoal lines refusing to coalesce into anything satisfying. A heavy silence pressed in, the kind that made the hairs on his arms prickle. He traced a line with his thumb, his jaw tight. Then, he saw it. A subtle shift in the shading of the corner—a pattern that wasn't his. It was a delicate, almost hidden script, woven into the texture of the shadow. A cold knot tightened in his chest. He leaned closer, his breath catching. It wasn't a mistake; it was deliberate. Someone had marked his creation. A low, almost inaudible sound escaped his throat.

[story 2]

I kept my gaze fixed on the unfinished sculpture, the marble cold beneath my fingertips, mirroring the emptiness inside me. The afternoon light slanted through the dusty window, illuminating the fine dust motes dancing in the air. Everything felt muted, distant. As I adjusted the angle of the piece, I noticed it—a precise, intricate series of symbols etched into the base, almost invisible unless the light hit it just right. My knuckles whitened as I gripped the tool. It wasn't my touch. A sudden, sharp awareness pierced the quiet numbness. Who had dared to claim a piece of my solitude? I slowly backed away, the scrape of my worn boots on the floor sounding deafeningly loud.

[story 3]

The city outside was a smear of indifferent neon, reflecting dully off the glass of my apartment window. I hadn't moved much all day, just pacing the worn rug, the silence amplifying the erratic rhythm of my own pulse. When I finally turned back to the easel, the familiar shapes of my recent work seemed alien. Near the edge of a portrait, a new layer of ink was visible—a complex, swirling design that looked alien against the oil paint. A deep furrow settled between my brows. It felt like an intrusion, a silent challenge thrown into my carefully constructed isolation. I picked up a brush, the movement stiff, and approached the work with a measured, almost hostile caution.
